title: Nameless (comic story)

[[51 BBY/Legends|51]]–[[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]]<ref name="NamelessDate">[[Nameless (comic story)|"Nameless"]] takes place during [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul's]] [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprenticeship]] to [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], the beginning of which occurs in ''[[The Wrath of Darth Maul]]'', and depicts the construction of [[Darth Maul's double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|Darth Maul's double-bladed lightsaber]], which is in use during the events of ''[[Darth Maul: Saboteur]]''. ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the relevant section of the former to [[51 BBY/Legends|51 BBY]], and the latter to [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]]. Therefore, "Nameless" must take place between the two [[Standard year/Legends|years]].</ref>

"'''Nameless'''" is an 8-page comic story that appeared in [[Star Wars Tales 10|''Star Wars Tales'' 10]] and was collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 3]]'' and ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: Rise of the Sith Vol. 1]]''. The story was written by [[Christian Read]], art by [[Chris Slane]], colors by [[Giulia Brusco]] and [[Matt Hollingsworth]], letters by [[Jason Hvam]] and edited by [[Diana Schutz]].

==Plot summary==
[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] questions his apprentice [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] to explain the story behind his new [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]].

Maul was ordered to the [[Jentares system/Legends|Jentares system]] by Sidious to kill the Jedi master [[Siolo Ur Manka]]. But Manka easily defeated Maul's surprise attack, using only a wooden staff.

Having fled, Maul regrouped and constructs his new weapon. In the next fight Maul surprises Manka, stabbing him through the back with the second blade.

Sidious encourages Maul to name the weapon. However Maul does not want it to become anything more than an instrument of murder—and nameless.

==Continuity==
This story had originally been deemed non-[[canon]].<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=840#15679046|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-09-21|time=2:45 PM}}</ref>

Although there is a reference to Siolo Ur Manka in ''[[The Dark Forces Saga]]'', there are a number of continuity discrepancies between this tale and the events of the [[Star Wars Journal: Darth Maul|''Star Wars Episode I Journal: Darth Maul'']].
